ddls:
CREATE TABLE "locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the monitoring location */,
  "city" TEXT /* City where the location is situated */,
  "state" TEXT /* State where the location is situated */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country where the location is situated */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the location */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the location */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for inquiries about the location */,
  "altitude" REAL /* Altitude of the monitoring station */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date when the monitoring station was installed */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the monitoring station (active, maintenance, decommissioned) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "pollutants" (
  "pollutant_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each pollutant */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the pollutant */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the pollutant */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the pollutant */,
  "health_impact" TEXT /* Health impact of the pollutant */,
  "regulatory_limit" REAL /* Regulatory limit for the pollutant */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("pollutant_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "measurements" (
  "measurement_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each measurement */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* ID of the location where the measurement was taken */,
  "pollutant_id" INTEGER /* ID of the pollutant being measured */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the measurement */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the measurement */,
  "instrument_id" INTEGER /* ID of the instrument used for the measurement */,
  "calibration_status" TEXT /* Calibration status of the instrument at the time of measurement */,
  "weather_condition" TEXT /* Weather conditions during the measurement */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("measurement_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_measurements_pollutant_id FOREIGN KEY ("pollutant_id") REFERENCES pollutants ("pollutant_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_measurements_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., data collector, analyst, admin) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last login */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the system */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the system was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., login, logout, view data) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the system was accessed */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the browser or device used */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who generated the report */,
  "report_name" TEXT /* Name of the report */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* Type of the report (e.g., daily, monthly, annual) */,
  "generation_date" TEXT /* Date when the report was generated */,
  "file_path" TEXT /* File path to the report */,
  "report_status" TEXT /* Status of the report (pending, completed, failed) */,
  "parameters_included" TEXT /* List of parameters included in the report */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
